---
title: "Overview"
---

{{< include _disclaimer.qmd >}}

# LEVANTE longitudinal analyses

An exploratory analysis of the **LEVANTE core-task battery** — a sequence of
reproducible notebooks that walk from raw data validation through construct
structure to within-child variability. Data come from `levante_data_latest` on
Redivis (currently the bug-fixed **v1.2** release) via the `rlevante` package;
shared conventions, palettes, and loaders live in `common.R`.

Each chapter computes its results inline from the data, so every number and
figure is current with the code that produced it.

## The arc

**Data & integrity** — pull and clean the data ([00](00_load_data.qmd)); audit
per-(site, task) integrity, adaptive/mode artifacts, and extreme scores
([01](01_data_integrity.qmd)).

**Growth & construct structure** — longitudinal traces and simple growth
([02](02_growth.qmd)); the g-vs-domains structure and cross-site measurement
invariance ([03](03_structure_invariance.qmd)); emergent dimensions,
age-differentiation, and the role of *g* with ROAR + MEFS as real measures
([04](04_differentiation.qmd)); and how short the battery can be while still
recovering the factor scores ([05](05_battery_design.qmd)).

**Within-child variability** — a first pass at three consistency indices and
whether they cohere ([06](06_within_child_variability.qmd)); a deep dive on
**RT intra-individual variability** — purification, cross-task structure,
site/age, and longitudinal stability ([07](07_rt_variability.qmd)); and the
**accuracy** companion using MSSD, and why nothing survives de-confounding
([08](08_accuracy_variability.qmd)).

**Network context** — enrollment ages and longitudinal coverage across the
LEVANTE network ([09](09_longitudinal_coverage.qmd)).

## A few headline findings

- A **column-order IRT scoring bug** in an earlier data release mis-scored the
  CAT/guessing-floor items; found, validated, fixed upstream (data re-released as
  v1.2). With corrected scores, apparent longitudinal declines disappear.
- The core battery is a **strong general factor** with weakly-separable domains
  that **differentiate with age**; structure replicates across Germany, Bogotá,
  and Canada.
- The battery is **highly compressible** — administration time can be roughly
  halved while still recovering every construct at ≈ 0.9 determinacy.
- **Within-child variability** is elusive: RT variability carries a real but
  narrow, speeded-task signal (cleanest after detrending item difficulty);
  accuracy variability, properly de-confounded, is essentially ability in
  disguise.

## Reproducibility & provenance

The notebooks render from Redivis data (gitignored; access via `rlevante`).
`common.R` is sourced by every chapter. This book was assembled with
[Quarto](https://quarto.org); see the
[source repository](https://github.com/levante-framework/levante-longitudinal).

This is **internal, exploratory work of the LEVANTE Data Coordinating Center** —
please read the two banners above before using anything here.
